headline: Kadazan Language Curriculum Reaches Only 180 Schools
context: Despite being Sabah largest indigenous group at 750,000 people Kadazandusun classes offered in only 180 of 1,400 primary schools.

cards.big: Only 180 of 1,400 Sabah schools offer Kadazandusun language classes
cards.sub: UNESCO classified the language as vulnerable with declining youth fluency

cards.big: Survey found only 38% of Kadazan youth under 25 are fluent speakers
cards.sub: Rate drops to 22% in urban centres like Kota Kinabalu and Sandakan

cards.big: Education Ministry allocated RM4.2 million for indigenous language in 2026
cards.sub: Amount covers only 95 trained teachers for 18 indigenous languages statewide

cards.big: KadazanDusun Cultural Association trained 200 volunteer teachers since 2023
cards.sub: Community-led weekend classes in 45 villages fill gaps left by formal education

cards.big: Language loss measures whether cultural preservation is funded or forgotten
Only 180 schools teach Kadazan. Language survival requires institutional commitment, not symbolic gestures.
